**Nathan Donald Diaz** (born April 16, 1985) is an American [mixed martial artist](/wiki/Mixed_martial_artist "Mixed martial artist") and [professional boxer](/wiki/Professional_boxer "Professional boxer")[[7]](#cite_note-7) who is currently a [free agent](/wiki/Free_agent "Free agent"). Diaz is most known for his time spent fighting in the [Ultimate Fighting Championship](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighting_Championship "Ultimate Fighting Championship") (UFC), where he fought for over 15 years after winning *[The Ultimate Fighter 5](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_5 "The Ultimate Fighter 5")*. Prior to signing with the UFC, Diaz competed in [World Extreme Cagefighting](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting"), [Strikeforce](/wiki/Strikeforce_(mixed_martial_arts) "Strikeforce (mixed martial arts)"), and [Pancrase](/wiki/Pancrase "Pancrase"). Diaz has the third most UFC bonus awards, with 16 in total.[[8]](#cite_note-8) In 2012 he challenged for the [UFC Lightweight Championship](/wiki/UFC_Lightweight_Championship "UFC Lightweight Championship").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Background

The son of Melissa (née Womble) and Robert Diaz, he was born of [Mexican](/wiki/Mexican_Americans "Mexican Americans") and [Anglo](/wiki/Anglo#United_States "Anglo") heritage and raised in [Stockton, California](/wiki/Stockton,_California "Stockton, California"), with his brother [Nick](/wiki/Nick_Diaz "Nick Diaz") and sister Nina.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11) He grew up in [Lodi, California](/wiki/Lodi,_California "Lodi, California")[[12]](#cite_note-12) and attended [Tokay High School](/wiki/Tokay_High_School "Tokay High School"). At the age of 11, he began training in [martial arts](/wiki/Martial_arts "Martial arts") with his brother, Nick.

## Mixed martial arts career

### Early career

Prior to his official MMA career, Diaz competed in a bare knuckle MMA fight against Robert Limon on July 20, 2002, at the age of 17.[[13]](#cite_note-13) Footage has since emerged of the fight and it is believed to be his first competitive experience.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

Upon making his official professional MMA debut, Diaz competed mainly for [World Extreme Cagefighting](/wiki/World_Extreme_Cagefighting "World Extreme Cagefighting") (WEC). In 2006, at [WEC 24](/wiki/WEC_24 "WEC 24"), he fought for the [WEC Lightweight Championship](/wiki/WEC_Lightweight_Championship "WEC Lightweight Championship") against then-champion [Hermes França](/wiki/Hermes_Fran%C3%A7a "Hermes França"), losing by submission in the second round. This was the last event held by WEC before it was acquired by [Zuffa, LLC](/wiki/Zuffa,_LLC "Zuffa, LLC"), then parent company of the UFC.

### Ultimate Fighting Championship

#### *The Ultimate Fighter 5*

Diaz was a contestant on *[The Ultimate Fighter 5](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter_5 "The Ultimate Fighter 5")*, which exclusively featured [lightweights](/wiki/Lightweight_(MMA) "Lightweight (MMA)"), fighting on [Jens Pulver's](/wiki/Jens_Pulver "Jens Pulver") team. In the preliminary round, Diaz defeated [Rob Emerson](/wiki/Rob_Emerson "Rob Emerson") by submission; in the quarterfinals he defeated fellow Team Pulver teammate [Corey Hill](/wiki/Corey_Hill "Corey Hill") via [triangle choke](/wiki/Triangle_choke "Triangle choke") submission in the first round. In the semifinals, Diaz defeated Team Penn member [Gray Maynard](/wiki/Gray_Maynard "Gray Maynard") by submission, advancing to the finals, in which he faced teammate [Manvel Gamburyan](/wiki/Manvel_Gamburyan "Manvel Gamburyan"). Diaz won by submission after Gamburyan was forced to tap out in the second round due to the dislocation of his right shoulder as the result of attempting a takedown.[[15]](#cite_note-15) With the victory, Diaz won *The Ultimate Fighter 5*.[[16]](#cite_note-16) This fight also earned him a $50,000 *[Knockout of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[17]](#cite_note-TUF5finals-17)

#### 2008

Diaz faced [Alvin Robinson](/wiki/Alvin_Robinson "Alvin Robinson") at [UFC Fight Night 12](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_12 "UFC Fight Night 12") on January 23, 2008. This fight earned him a $40,000 *[Submission of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[18]](#cite_note-UFCFN12-18)

After defeating [Alvin Robinson](/wiki/Alvin_Robinson "Alvin Robinson") and [Junior Assunção](/wiki/Junior_Assun%C3%A7%C3%A3o "Junior Assunção") via submission, Diaz demanded tougher opponents. He was given a match with [Kurt Pellegrino](/wiki/Kurt_Pellegrino "Kurt Pellegrino") at *[UFC Fight Night 13](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_13 "UFC Fight Night 13")*. Diaz defeated Pellegrino via triangle-choke submission in the second round. As he locked up the choke, Diaz had time to flex for the crowd and throw up double middle fingers before Pellegrino submitted. This fight earned him a $20,000 *[Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[19]](#cite_note-UFCFN13-19) Diaz then defeated veteran [Josh Neer](/wiki/Josh_Neer "Josh Neer") by split decision in the main event of *[UFC Fight Night 15](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_15 "UFC Fight Night 15")*. This fight earned him a $30,000 *[Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[20]](#cite_note-UFCFN5-20)

#### 2009

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/8d/Nate_Diaz_2009.jpg/250px-Nate_Diaz_2009.jpg)](/wiki/File:Nate_Diaz_2009.jpg)

Diaz in 2009.

Diaz next fought former [Strikeforce Lightweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_Strikeforce_champions#Lightweight_Championship "List of Strikeforce champions") [Clay Guida](/wiki/Clay_Guida "Clay Guida") at *[UFC 94: St. Pierre vs. Penn 2](/wiki/UFC_94:_St._Pierre_vs._Penn_2 "UFC 94: St. Pierre vs. Penn 2")*, losing via split decision. Guida used his [wrestling](/wiki/Wrestling "Wrestling") to take Diaz down multiple times. Guida won via split decision, marking Diaz's first loss in the UFC, in what was also his [pay per view](/wiki/Pay_per_view "Pay per view") debut. This fight earned him a $65,000 *[Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[21]](#cite_note-UFC94-21)

Diaz met fellow [Ultimate Fighter](/wiki/Ultimate_Fighter "Ultimate Fighter") winner and former [King of the Cage Welterweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_KOTC_champions "List of KOTC champions") [Joe Stevenson](/wiki/Joe_Stevenson "Joe Stevenson") at *[The Ultimate Fighter 9 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_United_States_vs._United_Kingdom_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: United States vs. United Kingdom Finale")*. Stevenson took Diaz down at will throughout the 3 rounds and Diaz lost by unanimous decision. This fight earned him a $25,000 *[Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[22]](#cite_note-TUF9-22)

After two consecutive losses by decision, Diaz headlined *[UFC Fight Night 19](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_19 "UFC Fight Night 19")* opposite [Melvin Guillard](/wiki/Melvin_Guillard "Melvin Guillard"). Diaz won by submission to a [guillotine choke](/wiki/Guillotine_choke "Guillotine choke") at 2:30 of round 2. This fight earned him a $30,000 *[Fight of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award.[[23]](#cite_note-UFCFN19-23)

#### 2010

Diaz faced [Gray Maynard](/wiki/Gray_Maynard "Gray Maynard") on January 11, 2010, in the main event at *[UFC Fight Night 20](/wiki/UFC_Fight_Night_20 "UFC Fight Night 20")*, a rematch from when the two met in the semi-finals of the *[TUF 5](/wiki/TUF_5 "TUF 5")* [lightweight](/wiki/Lightweight_(MMA) "Lightweight (MMA)") tournament, which Diaz won. Diaz lost a controversial split decision to Maynard, as the former TUF winner was given the nod in the FightMetric reports, winning rounds two and three.[[24]](#cite_note-24)[[25]](#cite_note-25)

After three losses in four fights, Nate considered making a permanent move up in weight to the 170 lb. weight class, stating "I don't make enough money to have to drop this much weight so I'd like to fight at 170 and only go to 155 every once in a while."[[26]](#cite_note-26)

Diaz made his [Welterweight](/wiki/Welterweight_(MMA) "Welterweight (MMA)") debut on March 27, 2010, at [UFC 111](/wiki/UFC_111 "UFC 111") against [Miletich Fighting Systems](/wiki/Miletich_Fighting_Systems "Miletich Fighting Systems") product and striking specialist [Rory Markham](/wiki/Rory_Markham "Rory Markham").[[27]](#cite_note-27) At the weigh-ins, Markham weighed in at 177, whereas Diaz weighed in at the [Welterweight](/wiki/Welterweight_(MMA) "Welterweight (MMA)") limit of 171; the fight was changed to a [catchweight](/wiki/Catchweight "Catchweight") fight. Diaz went on to win the fight by TKO in the first round.

After the win over Markham, Diaz stated he would compete in both weight classes. His next fight was again at Welterweight against former professional boxer [Marcus Davis](/wiki/Marcus_Davis "Marcus Davis") on August 28, 2010, at [UFC 118](/wiki/UFC_118 "UFC 118").[[28]](#cite_note-28) Diaz finished Davis after choking him unconscious with a [guillotine choke](/wiki/Guillotine_choke "Guillotine choke") submission in the final round; the bout earned them $60,000 *Fight of the Night* honors.[[29]](#cite_note-UFC118-29)

#### 2011

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3f/UFCWelterweightNateDiaz.jpg/250px-UFCWelterweightNateDiaz.jpg)](/wiki/File:UFCWelterweightNateDiaz.jpg)

Diaz at [UFC Live: Hardy vs. Lytle](/wiki/UFC_Live:_Hardy_vs._Lytle "UFC Live: Hardy vs. Lytle") in 2011

Diaz next faced South Korean fighter [Dong Hyun Kim](/wiki/Dong_Hyun_Kim "Dong Hyun Kim"), on January 1, 2011, at [UFC 125](/wiki/UFC_125 "UFC 125").[[30]](#cite_note-30) Diaz lost the fight via unanimous decision.

Diaz fought [Rory MacDonald](/wiki/Rory_MacDonald_(fighter) "Rory MacDonald (fighter)") on April 30, 2011, at [UFC 129](/wiki/UFC_129 "UFC 129").[[31]](#cite_note-31) Diaz was unable to mount much significant offense and lost via unanimous decision. Following the loss, Diaz stated that he would be moving back down to the lightweight division.[[32]](#cite_note-32)

Diaz defeated former [PRIDE Lightweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_Pride_champions "List of Pride champions") [Takanori Gomi](/wiki/Takanori_Gomi "Takanori Gomi") on September 24, 2011, at [UFC 135](/wiki/UFC_135 "UFC 135") via [armbar](/wiki/Armbar "Armbar") submission in the first round.[[33]](#cite_note-33) He earned a $75,000 *[Submission of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* award for his performance.[[34]](#cite_note-UFC135-34)

Diaz defeated [Donald Cerrone](/wiki/Donald_Cerrone "Donald Cerrone") at [UFC 141](/wiki/UFC_141 "UFC 141")[[35]](#cite_note-35) via unanimous decision (30–27, 30–27, and 29–28) in a performance that earned both participants $75,000 *Fight of the Night* honors.[[36]](#cite_note-UFC141-36) Despite being knocked off of his feet multiple times by sweeping leg kicks from Cerrone, Diaz had one of the best performances of his career. Diaz set a CompuStrike record, landing 82% of the strikes he threw en route to his victory over Cerrone.[[37]](#cite_note-37)

#### 2012

Diaz, after several years of training, finally received his [Brazilian jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu "Brazilian jiu-jitsu") [black belt](/wiki/Black_belt_(martial_arts) "Black belt (martial arts)") from Cesar Gracie.[[38]](#cite_note-38) He was awarded his black belt a month before his fight with fellow Brazilian jiu-jitsu black belt [Jim Miller](/wiki/Jim_Miller_(fighter) "Jim Miller (fighter)").

Diaz next faced [Jim Miller](/wiki/Jim_Miller_(fighter) "Jim Miller (fighter)") on May 5, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 3](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_3 "UFC on Fox 3").[[39]](#cite_note-39) Diaz outboxed Miller for the majority of the first two rounds, even dropping Miller with a straight left at the end of the first round. Near the end of round two, Miller shot in for a takedown, to which Diaz stuffed and countered with a rolling [guillotine choke](/wiki/Guillotine_choke "Guillotine choke"), forcing the tap at 4:09 of the second round. The submission won Diaz his fifth *[Submission of the Night](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients")* bonus award valued at $65,000.[[40]](#cite_note-UFCFox3-40) It was also the first time Miller had been stopped in his MMA career.[[41]](#cite_note-41)

Diaz faced [Benson Henderson](/wiki/Benson_Henderson "Benson Henderson") on December 8, 2012, at [UFC on Fox 5](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_5 "UFC on Fox 5") in the main event for the [UFC Lightweight Championship](/wiki/UFC_Lightweight_Championship "UFC Lightweight Championship").[[42]](#cite_note-42) Diaz lost the one-sided bout via unanimous decision.[[43]](#cite_note-43) Three weeks prior to the championship bout, Diaz signed an eight-fight contract with UFC.[[44]](#cite_note-44)

#### 2013

Diaz faced former [Strikeforce Lightweight Champion](/wiki/List_of_Strikeforce_champions#Lightweight_Championship "List of Strikeforce champions") [Josh Thomson](/wiki/Josh_Thomson "Josh Thomson") on April 20, 2013, at [UFC on Fox 7](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_7 "UFC on Fox 7"). He lost the fight via TKO due to a head kick and punches, during which Nate's brother, Nick Diaz physically threw a towel into the octagon, in an attempt to signal the referee to end the bout.[[45]](#cite_note-45)[[46]](#cite_note-46) On May 16, 2013, Diaz was suspended by the UFC for violating the UFC's code of conduct by using a homophobic slur on his [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter") page. The UFC expressed their disappointment and suspended him;[[47]](#cite_note-47) he was eventually suspended for 90 days and fined $20,000.[[48]](#cite_note-48)

A rubber match with [Gray Maynard](/wiki/Gray_Maynard "Gray Maynard") took place on November 30, 2013, in the main event at [The Ultimate Fighter 18 Finale](/wiki/The_Ultimate_Fighter:_Team_Rousey_vs._Team_Tate#The_Ultimate_Fighter_18_Finale "The Ultimate Fighter: Team Rousey vs. Team Tate").[[49]](#cite_note-49) Diaz won via TKO in the first round.[[50]](#cite_note-50) The stoppage earned him his first *Knockout of the Night* bonus.[[51]](#cite_note-51)

#### 2014

On May 5, 2014, both Nate Diaz and fellow UFC fighter [T. J. Grant](/wiki/T._J._Grant "T. J. Grant") were removed from the UFC's lightweight rankings after being deemed inactive by the UFC.[[52]](#cite_note-52)

After over a year away from the sport, Diaz returned from his self-imposed hiatus and faced [Rafael Dos Anjos](/wiki/Rafael_Dos_Anjos "Rafael Dos Anjos") on December 13, 2014, at [UFC on Fox 13](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_13 "UFC on Fox 13").[[53]](#cite_note-53) Diaz missed weight, coming in at 160.6 pounds. He was given additional time to make the lightweight limit, and cut down to 155.2. However, he was still fined 20 percent of his purse, which went to Rafael dos Anjos. Diaz, who seemed unmotivated and annoyed for most of the fight, lost the one-sided fight via unanimous decision (30–26, 30–26, and 30–27).[[54]](#cite_note-54)

#### 2015

Diaz was briefly linked to a welterweight bout with [Matt Brown](/wiki/Matt_Brown_(fighter) "Matt Brown (fighter)") on July 11, 2015, at [UFC 189](/wiki/UFC_189 "UFC 189").[[55]](#cite_note-55) However, in mid-April, Brown announced that the pairing had been scrapped.[[56]](#cite_note-56)

After sitting out again for over a year, Diaz returned to face [Michael Johnson](/wiki/Michael_Johnson_(fighter) "Michael Johnson (fighter)") on December 19, 2015, at [UFC on Fox 17](/wiki/UFC_on_Fox_17 "UFC on Fox 17").[[57]](#cite_note-57) He won the fight via unanimous decision and went on to give one of the most infamous post-fight interviews in UFC history, where he called out [Conor McGregor](/wiki/Conor_McGregor "Conor McGregor") by saying "you're taking everything I work for, motherfucker, and I'ma fight your fucking ass" on live broadcast television.[[58]](#cite_note-58) Both participants were awarded $50,000 *Fight of the Night* honors.[[59]](#cite_note-UFCFox17-59)

#### 2016

Diaz was set as a replacement to face [Conor McGregor](/wiki/Conor_McGregor "Conor McGregor") on March 5, 2016, at [UFC 196](/wiki/UFC_196 "UFC 196"), filling in for an injured [Rafael dos Anjos](/wiki/Rafael_dos_Anjos "Rafael dos Anjos").[[60]](#cite_note-60) With Diaz only having eleven days notice, the fight took place at welterweight (170 lbs) due to lack of time to cut weight.[[61]](#cite_note-61) Diaz won the fight via submission in the second round.[[62]](#cite_note-62) This gave Diaz his ninth submission victory in the UFC, tied for the second-most all-time behind only [Royce Gracie](/wiki/Royce_Gracie "Royce Gracie"). Both fighters were awarded *Fight of the Night* honors and Diaz was also awarded with the *Performance of the Night* bonus which totalled $100,000 overall.[[63]](#cite_note-UFC196-63)

A rematch with McGregor was scheduled for July 9, 2016, at [UFC 200](/wiki/UFC_200 "UFC 200").[[64]](#cite_note-64)[[65]](#cite_note-65) However, on April 19, the UFC announced that McGregor had been pulled from the event after failing to fulfill media obligations in Las Vegas related to the fight.[[66]](#cite_note-66) In turn, the fight with McGregor was rescheduled and took place the following month, contested again at welterweight, at [UFC 202](/wiki/UFC_202 "UFC 202").[[67]](#cite_note-67) Diaz lost the rematch via majority decision.[[68]](#cite_note-68) The contest was once again awarded *Fight of the Night* honors.[[69]](#cite_note-UFC202-69)

At the UFC 202 post-fight press conference, Diaz violated the [USADA](/wiki/USADA "USADA") rule by smoking CBD, [Cannabidiol](/wiki/Cannabidiol "Cannabidiol"), vape pen within four hours after a fight. He was not sanctioned as CBD is not considered a [PED](/wiki/Performance-enhancing_substance "Performance-enhancing substance"). USADA changed their rules and lifted CBD from the banned list not long after.[[70]](#cite_note-The_Inquisitr-70)

#### 2018

After a two-year-long layoff, it was announced on August 3, 2018, that Diaz was expected to return on November 3 against [Dustin Poirier](/wiki/Dustin_Poirier "Dustin Poirier") in a lightweight bout. This bout was expected to serve as the co-headliner of [UFC 230](/wiki/UFC_230 "UFC 230").[[71]](#cite_note-71) However, on October 9, 2018, it was announced that Poirier pulled out due to injury and as a result, the bout was canceled.[[72]](#cite_note-72)

#### 2019

Nate Diaz returned to competition on August 17 at [UFC 241](/wiki/UFC_241 "UFC 241"). He faced [Anthony Pettis](/wiki/Anthony_Pettis "Anthony Pettis") in a welterweight bout which served as the co-main event.[[73]](#cite_note-73) With a dominant performance, he won the fight by unanimous decision.[[74]](#cite_note-74)

Diaz faced [Jorge Masvidal](/wiki/Jorge_Masvidal "Jorge Masvidal") on November 2, 2019, in the main event of [UFC 244](/wiki/UFC_244 "UFC 244").[[75]](#cite_note-75) In a unique situation, UFC President [Dana White](/wiki/Dana_White "Dana White") confirmed that the headlining bout was for a celebratory "baddest motherfucker" ('BMF') belt.[[76]](#cite_note-76) After being dominated for most of the fight, Diaz lost the fight via TKO between rounds three and four when the cage-side physician determined a cut over Diaz's right eye rendered him unable to continue.[[77]](#cite_note-77)

#### 2021

Diaz was expected to face [Leon Edwards](/wiki/Leon_Edwards "Leon Edwards") on May 15, 2021, at [UFC 262](/wiki/UFC_262 "UFC 262") in the event's co-headliner, marking the first time a non-main event and non-title bout had been scheduled for 5 rounds.[[78]](#cite_note-78) However, the bout was moved to [UFC 263](/wiki/UFC_263 "UFC 263") due to a minor injury sustained by Diaz and eventually took place for five rounds as well.[[79]](#cite_note-79) Edwards controlled a vast majority of the bout, but was stunned badly with punches late in the fifth. Edwards won the fight by unanimous decision (49–46, 49–46, 49–46).[[80]](#cite_note-80)

#### 2022

On the last fight of his UFC contract, Diaz was scheduled to face [Khamzat Chimaev](/wiki/Khamzat_Chimaev "Khamzat Chimaev") on September 10, 2022, in the main event of [UFC 279](/wiki/UFC_279 "UFC 279").[[81]](#cite_note-Diaz_vs._Chimaev-81) However, at the weigh-ins on September 9, Chimaev weighed in at 178.5 pounds, seven-and-a-half pounds over the welterweight non-title fight limit.[[82]](#cite_note-82) As a result of Chimaev missing weight, he was removed from his bout with Diaz. Diaz instead faced [Tony Ferguson](/wiki/Tony_Ferguson "Tony Ferguson") in the main event.[[83]](#cite_note-83) Diaz won the fight via a guillotine choke submission in the fourth round.[[84]](#cite_note-84) This win earned Diaz his second [*Performance of the Night*](/wiki/List_of_UFC_bonus_award_recipients "List of UFC bonus award recipients") bonus award.[[85]](#cite_note-UFC279-85) Diaz opted to not re-sign with the UFC before the bout with Ferguson, thus making Diaz a free agent once the fight concluded.[[86]](#cite_note-86)

### Return

#### 2026

On March 16, 2026, it was announced that Diaz would make his return to MMA after a four-year hiatus under [Most Valuable Promotions](/wiki/Most_Valuable_Promotions "Most Valuable Promotions"), against [Mike Perry](/wiki/Mike_Perry_(fighter) "Mike Perry (fighter)") on May 16, 2026, at the [Intuit Dome](/wiki/Intuit_Dome "Intuit Dome") in [Inglewood, California](/wiki/Inglewood,_California "Inglewood, California").[[87]](#cite_note-87)

## Boxing career

### Diaz vs. Paul

Main article: [Jake Paul vs. Nate Diaz](/wiki/Jake_Paul_vs._Nate_Diaz "Jake Paul vs. Nate Diaz")

After becoming a free agent, Diaz decided to enter into the sport of boxing. On April 12, 2023, it was announced that Diaz would be making his professional boxing debut against [Jake Paul](/wiki/Jake_Paul "Jake Paul"). The bout took place on August 5, 2023, at the [American Airlines Center](/wiki/American_Airlines_Center "American Airlines Center") in [Dallas, Texas](/wiki/Dallas,_Texas "Dallas, Texas").[[88]](#cite_note-:2-88) Paul defeated Diaz via [unanimous decision](/wiki/Unanimous_decision "Unanimous decision") with scores of 98–91, 98–91, and 97–92.[[89]](#cite_note-89)[[90]](#cite_note-90)

### Diaz vs. Masvidal

On March 13, 2024, it was announced that Diaz would face [Jorge Masvidal](/wiki/Jorge_Masvidal "Jorge Masvidal") on June 1, 2024, at the [Kia Forum](/wiki/Kia_Forum "Kia Forum") in [Los Angeles, California](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles")[[91]](#cite_note-91) in a 10-round bout at light heavyweight.[[92]](#cite_note-92) On May 7, 2024, it was reported that the fight was rescheduled for July 6, 2024 at [Honda Center](/wiki/Honda_Center "Honda Center") in [Anaheim, CA](/wiki/Anaheim,_CA "Anaheim, CA").[[93]](#cite_note-93)[[94]](#cite_note-94)[[95]](#cite_note-95) On May 13, 2024, the new event date was confirmed for July 6, 2024.[[96]](#cite_note-96)[[97]](#cite_note-97) Diaz won the bout by majority decision.[[98]](#cite_note-98)[[99]](#cite_note-99)

After the boxing match with Jorge Masvidal, Diaz launched a lawsuit against Fanmio and its owner, Solomon Engel for fraud and breach of contract; alleging that he was only paid $1 million out of the total $10 million purse he was promised.[[100]](#cite_note-100)

## Personal life

Diaz's older brother [Nick](/wiki/Nick_Diaz "Nick Diaz") is also a professional MMA fighter.[[70]](#cite_note-The_Inquisitr-70) Both Diaz brothers are advocates for [cannabis](/wiki/Cannabis "Cannabis") and even have a licensed line of marijuana pre-rolls made by California Finest.[[101]](#cite_note-101)[[102]](#cite_note-102)[[103]](#cite_note-103)[[104]](#cite_note-104) They currently run a [Brazilian jiu-jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_jiu-jitsu "Brazilian jiu-jitsu") school in [Stockton, California](/wiki/Stockton,_California "Stockton, California") (Nick Diaz Academy), and founded a CBD company called Game Up Nutrition.[[105]](#cite_note-105) He has been primarily [vegan](/wiki/Veganism "Veganism") since he was 18 years old,[[106]](#cite_note-106)[[107]](#cite_note-107) but admits to eating fish [from time to time](/wiki/Flexitarianism "Flexitarianism").[[108]](#cite_note-108) On June 20, 2018, it was announced that Diaz's girlfriend had given birth to their daughter.[[109]](#cite_note-109)

In August 2022, Diaz publicly endorsed fellow UFC veteran [BJ Penn](/wiki/B.J._Penn "B.J. Penn") in his run for governor for Hawaii.[[110]](#cite_note-110)

In September 2022, Diaz announced the news that he was starting his own combat sports promotion company, Real Fight Inc.[[111]](#cite_note-111) Diaz remained connected to his roots, often giving back to his community in Stockton being involved with support for young fighters as he nurtures a new generation of talent.[[112]](#cite_note-112) As a result of the attention that Diaz had brought to the city of Stockton, he was presented with a key to the city by the mayor during a [Stockton Kings](/wiki/Stockton_Kings "Stockton Kings") basketball game in December 2022.[[113]](#cite_note-113)

In October 2022, Diaz was involved in a brawl backstage at the [Jake Paul vs. Anderson Silva](/wiki/Jake_Paul_vs._Anderson_Silva "Jake Paul vs. Anderson Silva") boxing match and was removed from the event as a result.[[114]](#cite_note-114)

In April 2023, footage emerged of Diaz getting involved in an altercation in the street that ended with him choking a man unconscious.[[115]](#cite_note-115) Diaz was then issued a warrant and Diaz turned himself in to the New Orleans Police Department on April 27, at 7:10 AM, facing a second-degree battery charge.[[116]](#cite_note-116) Diaz's attorney claimed that he acted in self-defense and he was released on a $10,000 bond.[[117]](#cite_note-117) On September 25, 2023, it was reported that the Orleans Parish would not pursue the case against Diaz indicating his actions were entirely in self defense as shown on video, clean in pictures and clear from the multiple witnesses.[[118]](#cite_note-118)

Diaz was involved in an alleged altercation with a security guard by pushing him in the face with "an open hand" on August 24, 2024 at Omnia nightclub at Caesars Palace while holding up the line. A misdemeanor battery charge was filed against Diaz in October. He could face up to six months in jail and a $1,000 fine if convicted.[[119]](#cite_note-119)[[120]](#cite_note-120)
